2605.11583.title: NexOP: Joint Optimization of NEX-Aware k-space Sampling and Image Reconstruction for Low-Field MRI
2605.11583.paper_summary: Modern low-field magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) technology offers a compelling alternative to standard high-field MRI, with portable, low-cost systems. However, its clinical utility is limited by a low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), which hampers diagnostic image quality. A common approach to increase SNR is through repetitive signal acquisitions, known as NEX, but this results in excessively long scan durations. Although recent work has introduced methods to accelerate MRI scans through k-space sampling optimization, the NEX dimension remains unexploited; typically, a single sampling mask is used across all repetitions. Here we introduce NexOP, a deep-learning framework for joint optimization of the sampling and reconstruction in multi-NEX acquisitions, tailored for low-SNR settings. NexOP enables optimizing the sampling density probabilities across the extended k-space-NEX domain, under a fixed sampling-budget constraint, and introduces a new deep-learning architecture for reconstructing a single high-SNR image from multiple low-SNR measurements. Experiments with raw low-field (0.3T) brain data demonstrate that NexOP consistently outperforms competing methods, both quantitatively and qualitatively, across diverse acceleration factors and tissue contrasts. The results also demonstrate that NexOP yields non-uniform sampling strategies, with progressively decreasing sampling across repetitions, hence exploiting the NEX dimension efficiently. Moreover, we present a theoretical analysis supporting these numerical observations. Overall, this work proposes a sampling-reconstruction optimization framework highly suitable for low-field MRI, which can enable faster, higher-quality imaging with low-cost systems and contribute to advancing affordable and accessible healthcare.
2605.11583.paper_summary_zh: 現代低場磁共振成像（MRI）技術提供了一個引人注目的替代方案，取代了標準的高場MRI，並且具有可攜帶、低成本的系統。<lb/>然而，其臨床效用受到低信號對噪聲比（SNR）的限制，這妨礙了診斷影像的質量。<lb/>增加SNR的一個常見方法是通過重複信號獲取，稱為NEX，但這會導致掃描時間過長。<lb/>雖然最近的研究已經引入了通過k空間採樣優化來加速MRI掃描的方法，但NEX維度仍未被充分利用；通常，所有重複中使用的是單一的採樣掩模。<lb/>在此，我們介紹NexOP，一個深度學習框架，用於在多NEX獲取中聯合優化採樣和重建，專為低SNR環境量身定制。<lb/>NexOP使得在固定採樣預算限制下，優化擴展k空間-NEX領域中的採樣密度概率，並引入了一種新的深度學習架構，從多個低SNR測量中重建單個高SNR影像。<lb/>使用原始低場（0.3T）腦部數據的實驗表明，NexOP在各種加速因子和組織對比下，始終優於競爭方法，無論是定量還是定性。<lb/>結果還顯示，NexOP產生了非均勻採樣策略，在重複中逐漸減少採樣，從而有效利用NEX維度。<lb/>此外，我們還提出了支持這些數值觀察的理論分析。<lb/>總體而言，這項工作提出了一個非常適合低場MRI的採樣-重建優化框架，能夠在低成本系統中實現更快、更高質量的成像，並有助於推進可負擔和可及的醫療保健。
2605.11583.author: Tal Oved et.al.
